segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Listen to a talk in an art history class.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Today, we're exploring the luminous world of stained glass.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: When you think of stained glass, you probably think of windows in churches, but it is found outside of churches as well.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: In fact, it used to brighten many homes across the United States.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: In the late 19th and early 20th centuries, stained glass windows became a beloved feature in domestic architecture, thanks to the Arts and Crafts movement.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: The Arts and Crafts movement celebrated craftsmanship and natural beauty, and stained glass fit the bill perfectly.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Artists use several techniques to bring their visions to life.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: The lead method involved joining pieces of glass with strips of lead, ideal for bold geometric designs.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Then came the copper foil technique, popularised by Louis Comfort Tiffany.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: This method wrapped each glass piece in thin copper foil, which allowed for more intricate designs like florals and landscapes.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: But these beautiful windows weren't without drawbacks.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: They often lack proper insulation, leading to heat loss in cold climates.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Homeowners had to weigh aesthetics against practicality, sometimes adding storm windows or heavy drapes to compensate.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Stained glass windows eventually faded from popularity in domestic architecture, but they're enjoying a quiet renaissance in contemporary art.

segments.speaker: Speaker
segments.text: Modern artists blend old techniques with new technologies, like laser cutting, digital design, and fused glass methods, to create contemporary pieces for homes, galleries and public spaces.
